How nervous system threat, hidden emotions, and protective stories can shape PIP at work.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

Photo by Sebastian Herrmann on UnsplashNot a friend of Medium? Click here to read for free.
When someone begins struggling at work or trying to reach their own goals, motivation is often the first thing we question.
Do they still care?
Are they taking the role seriously?
Have they become lazy, disengaged, or complacent?
Those questions are understandable, especially when missed deadlines, poor communication, inconsistent effort, or repeated mistakes are affecting other people.
It is understandable that a manager still has a responsibility to address the behavior.
The team cannot simply absorb the consequences of one person forever.
But motivation is not always the real problem.
Just like motivation to lose 30lbs is not the absolute problem.
Sometimes a person cares deeply and still cannot seem to change what they are doing. They may change for a short period of time, but after a few weeks or months they revert right back to how they were.
They may leave a meeting determined to improve, only to repeat the same pattern a…
